title: Media Narratives and Information Control
summary: Drop #13 continued Q's focus on media trust and public perception management. It framed mainstream narratives as coordinated influence operations.
raw_text:
Why does the media push narratives?
Why is this important?
Why attack alternative voices?
Who benefits?
Q

lines.fact: Media organizations and platforms regularly debate moderation standards, misinformation policies, and editorial credibility.
lines.theory: Q communities interpreted criticism or moderation of conspiracy content as coordinated suppression.
lines.context: Questions about free speech, misinformation, and platform moderation expanded significantly after 2016.
lines.questions: How can researchers distinguish moderation policies from coordinated censorship?
lines.Deltas: 2021-2025: Increased public debate over content moderation and deplatforming policies.
